binaries.title: hypervideo.sys - Uninitialized 7th argument to VMBus channel send on the bugcheck-display path (CWE-908)

changed_functions.name: CrashHvdSendMessage
key_changes: Added initialization of the 7th outgoing stack argument (mov [rsp+0x30], ebx with ebx=0) at 0x1C0001906 before the indirect dispatch through cs:__guard_dispatch_icall_fptr, which forwards to the VMBus channel-send function pointer rax=[rsi+0x10]. The unpatched build never wrote [rsp+0x30].
The ebp (arg5) store was rescheduled to after 'lea edx,[r8+1]' to accommodate the new instruction.
Loop-epilogue branch labels shifted by 4 bytes (jle 0x1C000191F -> 0x1C0001923, jmp 0x1C0001924 -> 0x1C0001928).
changed_functions.security_notes: The unpatched build sets up six outgoing arguments before the indirect call (rcx=*channel, edx=1, r8d=0, r9=packet data, [rsp+0x20]=packet size, [rsp+0x28]=0) and leaves the 7th-argument slot [rsp+0x30] unwritten. The patch adds 'mov [rsp+0x30], ebx' (ebx=0). This matches the use-of-uninitialized-memory pattern (CWE-908). Impact is Low and unconfirmed: the slot is a 32-bit dword (at most 4 bytes); the callee is an external function reached only through the channel object's function-pointer table (channel+0x10), so its true parameter count is not visible in these binaries and it cannot be shown that the slot is consumed. The function's only in-module callers are HvdDdiSystemDisplayWrite (0x1C0001490) and CrashHvdInitialize (0x1C0001764), i.e. the guest bugcheck/crash-display path (DxgkDdiSystemDisplayWrite), not routine rendering. The decompiled C is identical in both builds because the decompiler models the callee as a two-argument function pointer and cannot represent the extra stack arguments; the change is visible only in disassembly.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
VMBus channel send call site (addresses copied from the disassembly):

UNPATCHED:
00000001C00018FC  mov     rcx, [rsi]                 ; arg1 = *channel
00000001C00018FF  xor     r8d, r8d                   ; arg3 = 0
00000001C0001902  mov     rax, [rsi+10h]             ; callee fptr = channel[+10h]
00000001C0001906  mov     [rsp+48h+var_20], rbx      ; [rsp+28h] arg6 = 0
00000001C000190B  mov     [rsp+48h+var_28], ebp      ; [rsp+20h] arg5 = packet size
00000001C000190F  lea     edx, [r8+1]                ; arg2 = 1
00000001C0001913  call    cs:__guard_dispatch_icall_fptr   ; [rsp+30h] (arg7) never written

PATCHED:
00000001C00018FC  mov     rcx, [rsi]
00000001C00018FF  xor     r8d, r8d
00000001C0001902  mov     rax, [rsi+10h]
00000001C0001906  mov     [rsp+48h+var_18], ebx      ; [rsp+30h] arg7 = 0   <-- NEW
00000001C000190A  mov     [rsp+48h+var_20], rbx      ; [rsp+28h] arg6 = 0
00000001C000190F  lea     edx, [r8+1]                ; arg2 = 1
00000001C0001913  mov     [rsp+48h+var_28], ebp      ; [rsp+20h] arg5 = packet size
00000001C0001917  call    cs:__guard_dispatch_icall_fptr

disassembly frame ref is rsp+0x48, so var_18=[rsp+0x30], var_20=[rsp+0x28], var_28=[rsp+0x20].

changed_functions.name: HvdDdiStartDevice
key_changes: SynthVid IOCTL 0x3EC080 input grown from an 8-byte buffer {6,6} (InputBufferLength=8) to a 16-byte buffer {6,6,0,0} (InputBufferLength=0x10).
Stack frame grew 0x310 -> 0x320 (lea rbp,[rsp-0x210] -> [rsp-0x220]); the input buffer moved to [rbp+0x1b0].
The 16-byte buffer is zero-initialized near the function top at 0x1C00074EC ([rbp+0x1b0]) and 0x1C0007500 ([rbp+0x1b8]); the low qword is then set to 0x600000006 ({6,6}).
Neighboring call targets shift only because the frame grew (relocations, not behavioral changes).
changed_functions.security_notes: This is a protocol/functional change, not an information-disclosure fix. In BOTH builds every byte declared as IOCTL input is fully initialized: the unpatched build writes both dwords of its 8-byte buffer and declares InputBufferLength=8; the patched build zero-initializes all 16 bytes, sets the first qword to {6,6}, and declares InputBufferLength=0x10. Under METHOD_BUFFERED the receiver reads exactly InputBufferLength bytes; the remaining SystemBuffer space (up to OutputBufferLength=0x20) is the receiver's output scratch, not attacker-visible guest input. Therefore no uninitialized kernel memory is transmitted in either direction. The change simply extends the SynthVid message format from 8 to 16 bytes with the new fields set to 0.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
IOCTL construction (addresses copied from the disassembly):

UNPATCHED:
00000001C0007CF4  mov     eax, 6
00000001C0007D01  mov     [rsp+340h+InputBuffer], eax   ; bytes [0:3] = 6
00000001C0007D07  mov     [rsp+340h+var_2E4], eax       ; bytes [4:7] = 6
00000001C0007D25  lea     r8, [rsp+340h+InputBuffer]    ; 8-byte input, all written
00000001C0007D2F  mov     r9d, 8                        ; InputBufferLength = 8
00000001C0007D3A  mov     ecx, 3EC080h
00000001C0007D54  call    cs:__imp_IoBuildDeviceIoControlRequest

PATCHED:
00000001C00074EA  xor     eax, eax
00000001C00074EC  mov     [rbp+250h+InputBuffer], rax   ; bytes [0:7]  = 0
00000001C0007500  mov     [rbp+250h+var_98], rax        ; bytes [8:15] = 0
00000001C0007D02  mov     eax, 6
00000001C0007D0F  mov     dword ptr [rbp+250h+InputBuffer], eax     ; bytes [0:3] = 6
00000001C0007D17  mov     dword ptr [rbp+250h+InputBuffer+4], eax   ; bytes [4:7] = 6
00000001C0007D37  lea     r8, [rbp+250h+InputBuffer]    ; 16-byte input, all written
00000001C0007D43  mov     r9d, 10h                      ; InputBufferLength = 16
00000001C0007D4E  mov     ecx, 3EC080h
00000001C0007D68  call    cs:__imp_IoBuildDeviceIoControlRequest

disassembly frame ref is rbp+0x250, so InputBuffer=[rbp+0x1b0], var_98=[rbp+0x1b8].

security_findings.entry_point: Bugcheck/crash-display synthetic-video path. CrashHvdSendMessage is called only by HvdDdiSystemDisplayWrite (0x1C0001490, the DxgkDdiSystemDisplayWrite callback) and CrashHvdInitialize (0x1C0001764). The miniport callback table is published by DriverEntry (0x1C000C008).
call_chain: DriverEntry (0x1C000C008) - publishes the miniport callback table
CrashHvdSendMessage (0x1C00018B0) - VMBus channel send with the 7th argument uninitialized (unpatched)
security_findings.description: CrashHvdSendMessage dispatches an indirect call to the VMBus channel-send function pointer (rax=[rsi+0x10], rsi=[rcx+0x270]) through cs:__guard_dispatch_icall_fptr. Under the x64 calling convention the 7th argument is at [rsp+0x30]. The unpatched build sets up six outgoing arguments (rcx=*channel, edx=1, r8d=0, r9=packet data, [rsp+0x20]=packet size, [rsp+0x28]=0) and never writes [rsp+0x30]. The patch inserts 'mov [rsp+0x30], ebx' (ebx=0) at 0x1C0001906. This is a genuine CWE-908 initialization change with correct direction (patched is stricter). Impact is Low and unconfirmed: the slot is a 32-bit dword and the callee is external (VMBus channel library), reached only via the channel object's function-pointer table, so it cannot be shown from these binaries that the 7th slot is consumed.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: Reached on the guest bugcheck/crash-display flow (DxgkDdiSystemDisplayWrite paints the screen during a bugcheck) and via CrashHvdInitialize, not by ordinary window/mouse/rendering activity. When reached, the [rsp+0x30] slot is passed on each of up to 0x4E20 (20000) retry iterations (50 us KeStallExecutionProcessor between attempts).
security_findings.primitive: Initialization of a previously-unwritten 32-bit outgoing argument slot. If the external callee reads a 7th parameter, the unpatched build passed up to 4 bytes of uninitialized kernel-stack data (guest -> host); whether it does so cannot be confirmed from this module. Read-only condition at most (no write, no UAF, no type confusion).
security_findings.evidence: The change is visible only in disassembly; the decompiled C is byte-identical in both builds because the decompiler models the callee as a two-argument function pointer. See assembly below.
security_findings.assembly:
UNPATCHED (call site):
00000001C00018FC  mov     rcx, [rsi]                 ; arg1 = *channel
00000001C00018FF  xor     r8d, r8d                   ; arg3 = 0
00000001C0001902  mov     rax, [rsi+10h]             ; callee fptr = channel[+10h]
00000001C0001906  mov     [rsp+48h+var_20], rbx      ; [rsp+28h] arg6 = 0
00000001C000190B  mov     [rsp+48h+var_28], ebp      ; [rsp+20h] arg5 = packet size
00000001C000190F  lea     edx, [r8+1]                ; arg2 = 1
00000001C0001913  call    cs:__guard_dispatch_icall_fptr   ; [rsp+30h] (arg7) never written

PATCHED (call site):
00000001C0001906  mov     [rsp+48h+var_18], ebx      ; [rsp+30h] arg7 = 0   <-- NEW
00000001C000190A  mov     [rsp+48h+var_20], rbx      ; [rsp+28h] arg6 = 0
00000001C000190F  lea     edx, [r8+1]                ; arg2 = 1
00000001C0001913  mov     [rsp+48h+var_28], ebp      ; [rsp+20h] arg5 = packet size
00000001C0001917  call    cs:__guard_dispatch_icall_fptr

breakpoints.why: Indirect dispatch (unpatched). Inspect the 7th-argument slot [rsp+0x30] here. In the patched build the corresponding call is at +0x1917 and [rsp+0x30] has been set to 0 at +0x1906.

watch: [rsp+0x30] at the dispatch site - unwritten in the unpatched build, 0 in the patched build
rax = [rsi+0x10] - the channel-send function pointer forwarded through __guard_dispatch_icall_fptr
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: Exercised on the guest bugcheck/crash-display path via HvdDdiSystemDisplayWrite (DxgkDdiSystemDisplayWrite) or CrashHvdInitialize.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: At the dispatch site, 'dd @rsp+0x30 l1' shows residual stack contents in the unpatched build and 0x00000000 in the patched build. Whether the callee reads this slot is external to this module.
debugging_aids.notes: The callee is dispatched via __guard_dispatch_icall_fptr to rax=[rsi+0x10]; its true parameter count is defined in the VMBus channel library and is not determinable from this driver. The retry loop counts down from 0x4E20 with 50 us stalls.

security_findings.entry_point: Display adapter start callback (registered by DriverEntry at 0x1C000C008 as miniport callback-table slot [2]).
call_chain: HvdDdiStartDevice (0x1C00074B0) - issues SynthVid IOCTL 0x3EC080 to the VMBus device object
security_findings.description: The prior draft claimed an uninitialized-pool disclosure via an undersized METHOD_BUFFERED InputBuffer. That is not supported by the binaries. In BOTH builds every byte declared as IOCTL input is fully initialized. The unpatched build writes both dwords of an 8-byte buffer {6,6} and declares InputBufferLength=8; the patched build zero-initializes a 16-byte buffer (0x1C00074EC and 0x1C0007500), sets the first qword to 0x600000006 ({6,6}), and declares InputBufferLength=0x10. Under METHOD_BUFFERED the receiver reads exactly InputBufferLength bytes; the remaining SystemBuffer space (up to OutputBufferLength=0x20) is the receiver's own output scratch, not attacker-visible guest input. The change is a SynthVid message-format extension from 8 to 16 bytes with the new fields set to 0.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: The IOCTL path is reached during adapter start after the version gate (cmp edx, 0x30004), channel creation (SynthVidCreate 0x1C000984C), version query (SynthVidRequestResolutions 0x1C0009CD0), mode negotiation, and a successful 0x20-byte ExAllocatePoolWithTag. No uninitialized data is transmitted in either build, so there is no security-relevant trigger.
security_findings.primitive: None. Both builds send fully-initialized input up to the declared InputBufferLength.
security_findings.evidence: Unpatched: _DWORD InputBuffer[2]; InputBuffer[0]=6; InputBuffer[1]=6; IoBuildDeviceIoControlRequest(0x3EC080, *(a1+8), InputBuffer, 8u, *(a1+624), 0x20u, 0, &Event, &IoStatusBlock). Patched: _QWORD InputBuffer[2]; InputBuffer[0]=0; InputBuffer[1]=0; ...; InputBuffer[0]=0x600000006LL; IoBuildDeviceIoControlRequest(0x3EC080, *(a1+8), InputBuffer, 0x10u, *(a1+624), 0x20u, 0, &Event, &IoStatusBlock).
security_findings.assembly:
UNPATCHED:
00000001C0007CF4  mov     eax, 6
00000001C0007D01  mov     [rsp+340h+InputBuffer], eax   ; bytes [0:3] = 6
00000001C0007D07  mov     [rsp+340h+var_2E4], eax       ; bytes [4:7] = 6
00000001C0007D25  lea     r8, [rsp+340h+InputBuffer]    ; 8-byte input, all written
00000001C0007D2F  mov     r9d, 8                        ; InputBufferLength = 8
00000001C0007D54  call    cs:__imp_IoBuildDeviceIoControlRequest

PATCHED:
00000001C00074EA  xor     eax, eax
00000001C00074EC  mov     [rbp+250h+InputBuffer], rax   ; bytes [0:7]  = 0
00000001C0007500  mov     [rbp+250h+var_98], rax        ; bytes [8:15] = 0
00000001C0007D0F  mov     dword ptr [rbp+250h+InputBuffer], eax     ; bytes [0:3] = 6
00000001C0007D17  mov     dword ptr [rbp+250h+InputBuffer+4], eax   ; bytes [4:7] = 6
00000001C0007D43  mov     r9d, 10h                      ; InputBufferLength = 16
00000001C0007D68  call    cs:__imp_IoBuildDeviceIoControlRequest

breakpoints.why: IoBuildDeviceIoControlRequest call site (unpatched; patched call is at +0x7d68). Inspect r9d (InputBufferLength) and the InputBuffer contents.

watch: r9d at the IoBuildDeviceIoControlRequest call - InputBufferLength = 8 (unpatched) vs 0x10 (patched)
[r8] input buffer - 06 00 00 00 06 00 00 00 (unpatched, 8 valid bytes) vs the same followed by eight zero bytes (patched, 16 valid bytes)
key_offsets: 0x1c00074c6: sub rsp, 0x310 (unpatched) / 0x320 (patched) - frame grew to hold the wider buffer
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: Reached during adapter start after the version and negotiation preconditions are met.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: Both builds present fully-initialized input up to the declared InputBufferLength; there is nothing uninitialized to observe.
debugging_aids.notes: IOCTL 0x3EC080: device type 0x3E, function 0x20, method METHOD_BUFFERED (0), access FILE_READ_DATA|FILE_WRITE_DATA (3). The output buffer is a 0x20-byte pool allocation at *(context+0x270).

summary: Two functions changed. Finding A (CrashHvdSendMessage, 0x1C00018B0): the patch adds initialization (=0) of the 7th outgoing stack argument at [rsp+0x30] before the indirect dispatch to the VMBus channel-send function pointer, which the unpatched build left unwritten. This matches CWE-908 with the correct direction, but its security impact is Low and unconfirmed - the slot is a 32-bit dword, the reachable path is the guest bugcheck/crash-display flow (HvdDdiSystemDisplayWrite / CrashHvdInitialize), and the callee is an external VMBus-library function reached via a function-pointer table, so it cannot be shown that the slot is consumed. Finding B (HvdDdiStartDevice, 0x1C00074B0): downgraded to No security-relevant change. Both builds fully initialize the SynthVid IOCTL 0x3EC080 input up to the declared InputBufferLength (8 bytes in the unpatched build, 16 in the patched build); the change is a message-format extension from 8 to 16 bytes with the new fields set to 0, not an information-disclosure fix.
